description: Text normalization accuracy evaluation protocol for NepTTS-Bench. Tests whether the text normalization pipeline correctly converts written-form text to spoken-form text before synthesis. Covers numbers, dates, currency, time, phone numbers, abbreviations, and percentages.

notes.usage: Run the normalizer on all test inputs and compare output to expected spoken forms via exact string match. Normalization is upstream of synthesis — errors here propagate to all downstream metrics. Test both in isolation and end-to-end.
notes.evaluation: A passing normalizer must achieve >= 98% exact match overall and >= 95% per category. Failures are categorized and analyzed to guide normalizer rule development.
notes.reference: Test data drawn from dual-purpose files in data/ which define both normalization rules and expected outputs.

test_sources.description: Dual-purpose files in data/ that serve both the normalizer pipeline and as benchmark test inputs. Each file contains input-output pairs for a normalization category.

numbers.description: Number-to-word conversion (cardinal, ordinal, decimal, large numbers)

abbreviations.description: Abbreviation expansion to full spoken forms

phone_numbers.description: Phone number digit-by-digit or grouped reading patterns

exact_match_accuracy.description: Percentage of test inputs where the normalizer output exactly matches the expected spoken form after whitespace normalization.

numbers.description: Cardinal numbers, ordinal numbers, decimal numbers, large numbers (lakh, crore)

dates.description: Date formats (BS and AD calendars, various separators)

currency.description: Currency amounts (NPR, USD, mixed formats with commas and decimals)

phone_numbers.description: Phone numbers (landline, mobile, with/without country code)

abbreviations.description: Common Nepali and English abbreviations (e.g., ने.रु., WHO, etc.)

coverage.description: Percentage of corpus utterances that trigger at least one normalization rule. Low coverage may indicate missing rules for common patterns.
coverage.notes: No pass/fail threshold — this is an informational metric to identify normalization gaps.

edge_cases.description: Specific challenging inputs that test normalizer robustness.

examples: रु. 1,234.56 (currency with comma grouping and decimals)
२०८१/०१/१५ मा रु. ५,००० (date + currency in same utterance)
बिहान ७:३० मा +977-1-4XXXXXX मा फोन गर्नुहोस् (time + phone number)

nested_normalizations.description: Inputs where one normalization produces text requiring further normalization
examples: Abbreviation expanding to text containing a number

ambiguous_formats.description: Inputs where the normalization depends on context

procedure: Extract all input-output pairs from the test source files
Run the text normalizer on each input
Compare normalized output to expected spoken form (exact match after whitespace normalization)
Classify each failure by category and error type (missing rule, incorrect rule, ambiguity)
Compute per-category and overall exact match accuracy
Run coverage analysis on the full corpus to identify untriggered normalization categories
Test edge cases and mixed-format inputs separately
Report failures with input, expected output, actual output, and error classification

aggregate_reporting.description: How to report normalizer accuracy results.
aggregate_reporting.pass_rate: Percentage of inputs with exact match

failure_report.description: For each failure, report: input text, expected output, actual output, error category, and whether the error would cause an audible mispronunciation
